sourceMovie.title: Lilo & Stitch
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Lilo & Stitch for the same reason it broke through in 2025: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a science fiction rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Dean Fleischer Camp keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The family and science fiction blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Home
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Lilo & Stitch clicked for you, Home (2015) is a strong next stop: both trade in family and science fiction, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Lilo & Stitch 2: Stitch Has a Glitch
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Lilo & Stitch clicked for you, Lilo & Stitch 2: Stitch Has a Glitch (2005) is a strong next stop: both trade in family and science fiction, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Lilo & Stitch?
faq.answer: Try Home, Lilo & Stitch 2: Stitch Has a Glitch, Elio—each shares DNA with Lilo & Stitch while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Lilo & Stitch?
faq.answer: Lilo & Stitch (2025) is best described as Family, Science Fiction, Comedy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Lilo & Stitch part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Lilo & Stitch (Live-Action) Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
